ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-24-2021 Continued To Apr-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gerard Launay appeared by zoom for James Yee.

Mr. Launay stated that Mr. Bresler is ill and that it is his understanding that there is a final issue with the accounting, The Court stated that there are more than 20 issues listed in the Probate Examiner's notes that have not been addressed, and expressed concern about the estate funds being held in one account and exceeding the FDIC insured limit. Mr. Launay stated that the Executor went to three banks, that the banks do not want to open an account that requires two signatures, that the Executor will go to a couple more banks and that he may have to amend the letters to show one Executor or the other. The Court stated that it will not do that and that one alternative is to have a bond. Mr. Launay stated that his hands are tied, that he just got notice yesterday, raised the issue of replacing the Executors and asked to be allowed 30-days to figure this out. The Court stated that its thoughts go out to Mr. Bresler, that it will continue this one time, and that if the issues are not resolved, this petition will go off calendar and status on close of estate will be put on calendar. The Court once again expressed that it is very concerned about the account not being insured. The First and Final Report and Account of Co-Executors, etc. is continued to April 21, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
